title: Explaining CLIP Zero-shot Predictions Through Concepts

method.name: EZPC
method.full_name: Explaining CLIP Zero-shot Predictions Through Concepts
method.approach: Projects CLIP image and text embeddings into a shared concept space using a learnable matrix

mechanism.concept_space: Projects CLIP embeddings into a shared concept space
mechanism.explanation: Provides concept-based explanations via Hadamard product
mechanism.prediction: Generates class logits via dot-product in concept space
mechanism.optimization: Jointly optimizes a matching loss and a reconstruction loss

implementation.type: Lightweight decomposition that doesn't require retraining CLIP

contributions: Bridges the gap between interpretability of Concept Bottleneck Models (CBMs) and generalization ability of CLIP
Provides faithful, concept-level explanations of CLIP's predictions while maintaining zero-shot capabilities
Avoids the need for task-specific training or annotated concepts

problem_addressed.clip_limitation: CLIP's embeddings are high-dimensional and entangled, offering little interpretability
cbm_limitations: Operate under closed-world assumption, failing to generalize to unseen categories
problem_addressed.previous_approaches: Still require task-specific training and cannot generalize to unseen classes
